source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In the $\mathrm{xOy}$ plane, there is a semicircular uniform magnetic field in the region $x > 0$. The center of the magnetic field region is at point O, with a radius of $R = 0.1\mathrm{m}$. The magnetic induction strength is $B = 0.5\mathrm{T}$, and its direction is perpendicular to the $\mathrm{xOy}$ plane, pointing inward. A linear particle source continuously emits positively charged particles from the left side of the y-axis, moving parallel to the positive direction of the $\mathbf{X}$ axis. The charge of the particles is $q = 1.6 \times 10^{-19}C$, their initial velocity is $v_0 = 1.6 \times 10^6 \mathrm{m/s}$, and their mass is $m = 1.0 \times 10^{-26}\mathrm{kg}$. The interactions between particles are neglected, and the gravitational force on the particles is ignored.

1. Find the radius of motion of these particles in the magnetic field.
2. Find the y-coordinate of the particle leaving the magnetic field region when it is incident from point O.
3. Find the maximum time these particles move in the magnetic field and the y-axis coordinate of the particle at the time of incidence.

issues: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths reference different image files.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate nontrivial visual dependence
The setup is explicitly spatial/graphical: xOy plane, semicircular magnetic-field region in x>0, center O, and asks for exit position and y-coordinates
asset_registry_record shows 1 existing image, consistent with a diagram-backed physics geometry problem
multi_step: The problem has 3 linked sub-questions: motion radius, exit y-coordinate for a specific incidence point, and maximum time in field with corresponding incidence y-coordinate
Solving requires combining charged-particle circular motion in a magnetic field with geometry of a semicircular boundary
The last part asks for a maximum time and the associated coordinate, which adds conditional/extremum reasoning beyond direct formula substitution
verifiability: The expected answers are concrete numeric quantities with units/signs, which are straightforward to check
All main physical parameters are given explicitly (q, m, v0, B, R), so the results can be recomputed from standard formulas and geometry
answer_present=true, registry_passed=true, and the provided answer is a compact set of values suitable for rule-based or symbolic verification

risk_flags: Multi-part answer is terse, so aligning each numeric value to the corresponding sub-question may require care
Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths reference different image files.

decision_reasons: Question text, answer, and an existing primary image are all present, so the sample retains its core semantic assets.
Initial scoring is strong on image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability, indicating a valuable candidate.
The noted image-path inconsistency is a metadata risk, but it does not by itself remove the recoverable problem content.

The charge of the particles is $q = 1.6 \times 10^{-19}\mathrm{C}$, their initial velocity is $v_0 = 1.6 \times 10^6\mathrm{m/s}$, and their mass is $m = 1.0 \times 10^{-26}\mathrm{kg}$. The interactions between particles are neglected, and the gravitational force on the particles is ignored.

1. Find the radius of motion of these particles in the magnetic field.
2. Find the y-coordinate of the particle leaving the magnetic field region when it is incident from point O.
3. Find the maximum time these particles move in the magnetic field and the y-axis coordinate of the particle at the time of incidence.

open_ended_problem_variants.title: PhysReason 开放题
open_ended_problem_variants.rewritten_question_text:
In the $\mathrm{xOy}$ plane, there is a semicircular uniform magnetic field in the region $x > 0$. The center of the magnetic field region is at point O, with a radius of $R = 0.1\mathrm{m}$. The magnetic induction strength is $B = 0.5\mathrm{T}$, and its direction is perpendicular to the $\mathrm{xOy}$ plane, pointing inward. A linear particle source continuously emits positively charged particles from the left side of the y-axis, moving parallel to the positive direction of the x-axis. The charge of the particles is $q = 1.6 \times 10^{-19}\mathrm{C}$, their initial velocity is $v_0 = 1.6 \times 10^6\mathrm{m/s}$, and their mass is $m = 1.0 \times 10^{-26}\mathrm{kg}$. The interactions between particles are neglected, and the gravitational force on the particles is ignored.

1. Find the radius of motion of these particles in the magnetic field.
2. Find the y-coordinate of the particle leaving the magnetic field region when it is incident from point O.
3. Find the maximum time these particles move in the magnetic field and the y-axis coordinate of the particle at the time of incidence.
